title: TLS 1.3, Certificate Identity, ALPN, and HTTPS · Execution Trace: Tls Policy Resumption Zero Rtt
summary: A service enables TLS without certificate-name validation, chooses HTTP after reading encrypted application bytes, allows unsafe versions, and treats encryption as authorization and content integrity proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tls policy resumption zero rtt increment in TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define supported versions, cipher and curve policy, session resumption, client authentication, and replay boundaries while treating early data explicitly.
Offer and select application protocols with ALPN before application data and reject unsupported or inconsistent negotiation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Tls Policy Resumption Zero Rtt · Theory Tls Policy Resumption Zero Rtt
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 3400 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTPS on certificate identity, TLS policy, ALPN, and explicit early-data review; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
